key_insight: Morphology engineering made an insulating chiral 2D perovskite laterally conductive enough to measure p-type Hall transport and magnetoresistance for the first time.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical device integration of chiral perovskites for spintronic and magnetotransport studies by linking thin-film microstructure to in-plane electrical performance.
method: Optimized thin-film morphology of R-(MBA)2PbI4 to reduce the in-plane resistance bottleneck.
Fabricated Hall-bar lateral devices for dark Hall and magnetoresistance measurements.
Compared transport paths that cross grain boundaries to isolate morphology effects.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports p-type conduction with Hall mobility of ~0.2 cm^2 V^-1 s^-1 and carrier density of ~3×10^14 cm^-2, plus enhanced magnetoresistance across grain boundaries.
watch_item: The reported mobility is still very low, so device performance may remain strongly limited by grain boundaries and film microstructure.
paper_title: Morphology control and low-temperature magnetotransport in chiral 2D perovskite R-(MBA)$_2$PbI$_4$
